params.text:
Recording and reporting a fall

Every fall must be recorded, even if the person seems unhurt – a fall without injury is still a fall, and the record helps identify patterns and prevent the next one. Follow your organisation's incident and accident reporting process, and update the person's care plan and risk assessment as needed.

  • Record the date, time and place, what happened and what care was given
  • Note the person's condition and any injury, and who was informed
  • Continue post-fall observations, as some effects of a fall show up hours later
  • Report through your accident/incident system so managers can review it

Some incidents are reportable to the HSE under RIDDOR (the Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations). For a person receiving care, this generally applies where a work-related fall means they are taken directly to hospital for treatment of an injury. Your manager will usually decide and make any RIDDOR report.

params.taskDescription: Complete this guidance on recording and reporting a fall. Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Record a fall even if there is no visible *injury*, because a fall without harm is still important information. Note the *time* and place, describe what happened, and continue post-fall *observations* afterwards. Update the person's care *plan* so future risk is reduced. Serious work-related incidents may also be *reportable* to the HSE under RIDDOR.

params.text:
Key points to remember

  • Slips and trips are the most common workplace injury – and almost always preventable through good housekeeping
  • Clean spills immediately, use signs, clear walkways and report defects
  • Falls in older and frail people are multifactorial; a previous fall is the biggest warning sign
  • Reduce risk through the environment, footwear, walking aids and keeping items within reach
  • If someone falls, stay calm, do not rush to move them, check for injury and summon help
  • Record every fall and report as your organisation and RIDDOR require
